title: Pit Window Gap Pressure Proxies
summary: Strategy models emphasize traffic, pit-loss windows, and undercut/overcut incentives.
rationale: Strategy models emphasize traffic, pit-loss windows, and undercut/overcut incentives. Prior attempts used coarse rival-ahead tyre life and field pit counts, but not whether a car would rejoin into traffic or has a free-air gap based on positions and lap-time deltas.
implementation_hint: For each Race-Lap sorted by Position, add features for neighbour context: position_ahead/behind lap-time deviation, tyre-life deltas to 1-2 cars ahead/behind, local pack density from adjacent lap-time gaps, and estimated pit-window flag such as Position_after_loss_proxy = Position + round(track_pit_loss / median_lap_gap_proxy) using race-level constants learned from pit-lap time anomalies.
expected_effect: Could improve ranking around strategic stop decisions where similar tyre age drivers differ by traffic incentives.
risk: The dataset lacks explicit time gaps, so proxies from lap times/positions may be weak or unstable.
